vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup gshadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/gshadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/gshadow-

triage_decision.reason: The finding is related to file permissions, which is a low-risk change. The recommended remediation is a simple chmod command, which is unlikely to cause issues.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Vulnerability openscap_031 not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

review_feedback: QA validation error: name 'datetime' is not defined Suggestions: Use the recommended chmod u-xwrs,g-xwrs,o-xwrt /etc/gshadow-; Validate that the resulting permissions meet system expectations before applying

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The fix sets permissions to 0000, which resolves the vulnerability but is more restrictive than the recommended chmod pattern; consider using the suggested chmod command for a more precise fix.
concerns: Overly restrictive 0000 permissions may be more restrictive than necessary
Remediation does not match the exact recommended chmod pattern
suggested_improvements: Validate that the resulting permissions meet system expectations before applying
